description: Canonical mapping from source data through InfoWatchtower news records to company intranet SQL.

source_config.purpose: Defines where data comes from and how it should be fetched.

lineage.path: daily_report_items -> generated_news -> recommendation_items -> dedupe_group_items -> news_items -> raw_items -> data_sources
lineage.rule: Editing and SQL export must preserve foreign keys back to news_items and raw_items.

generated_news_json.notes: sourceUrl and created are forced from the original news item, not trusted from the model. Runtime GeneratedNews stores content_json directly; exporter projects it back to the legacy content object.

company_sql.export_scope: Only published/adopted daily report items with generation_status=ready and non-rule_v1 generated content are exported by default. Candidate-only items, non-adopted retained items, and fallback_needs_review generated drafts stay inside InfoWatchtower unless an admin explicitly creates a special export job.
company_sql.safe_write_pattern: INSERT IGNORE ai_journal first; later tables use INSERT ... SELECT id FROM ai_journal WHERE source_url = :source_url LIMIT 1.
company_sql.datetime_literal_rule: ai_journal.created_at and ai_journal_analysis.created_at must keep the legacy generate_ai_sql.py column shape and literal style: a quoted MySQL datetime literal 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS'. The literal is rendered in Asia/Shanghai, matching daily report day_key selection; if the source item has no published time, standard daily export falls back to daily_reports.day_key 09:00:00 instead of writing NULL. Do not replace this with STR_TO_DATE/CAST and do not omit ai_journal_analysis.created_at unless the intranet importer contract is explicitly changed and revalidated.

content_json_export_shape.reason: Company intranet SQL expects legacy analysis.content only. InfoWatchtower lineage stays in local relational tables and must not be serialized into company content_json.

company_sql_templates.ai_journal: INSERT IGNORE INTO ai_journal (source_url, source_title, content, created_at) VALUES (:source_url, :source_title, :raw_content, :created_at);
company_sql_templates.ai_journal_focus: INSERT IGNORE INTO ai_journal_focus (journal_id, focus_id) SELECT id, :focus_id FROM ai_journal WHERE source_url = :source_url LIMIT 1;
company_sql_templates.ai_journal_analysis: INSERT INTO ai_journal_analysis (journal_id, category, title, summary, key_points, content_json, source_url, created_at) SELECT id, :category, :title, :summary, :key_points, :content_json, :source_url, :created_at FROM ai_journal WHERE source_url = :source_url LIMIT 1;
company_sql_templates.t_news_data_info: INSERT INTO t_news_data_info (catalog_id, journal_id, data, adoption_status, category, title, summary, key_points, content_json, source_url) SELECT NULL, id, NULL, :adoption_status, :category, :title, :summary, :key_points, :content_json, :source_url FROM ai_journal WHERE source_url = :source_url LIMIT 1;

export_validation_rules: default export query must start from published daily_report_items joined to generated_news/news_items/raw_items.
only daily_report_items with adoption_status == 2 are exported by the standard daily SQL job.
adopted items must point to generated_news.generation_status == ready and generated_by must not start with rule_v1.
category must be one of config/taxonomy/news_categories.json AI 10 first-level labels.
source tags from config/taxonomy/source_tags.json must not be exported as category.
source_url is required and should be checked against the company ai_journal.source_url max length before export.
title, summary, key_points, and content_json are required for ai_journal_analysis and t_news_data_info.
ai_journal.source_title and ai_journal.content must be plain text in exported SQL; raw_items.raw_payload_json and raw_items.raw_content preserve the original unmodified payload for lineage.
ai_journal.created_at and ai_journal_analysis.created_at should preserve original published time; do not redistribute dates.

created_at literals must match the old validated SQL shape: quoted 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS', rendered in Asia/Shanghai, with no STR_TO_DATE/CAST wrapper and no omitted ai_journal_analysis.created_at column. Missing source published_at falls back to daily_reports.day_key 09:00:00 for import stability.

Every SQL preview must pass scripts/validate_company_sql.py before it is imported into the intranet. The script validates the 4-table order, exact column lists, field-level value rules, source_url lineage, matching created_at fields, content_json five-key shape, no HTML tags, no NULL created_at, and no STR_TO_DATE/CAST wrapper.
SQL preview headers must use the canonical InfoWatchtower Company SQL Preview title block. Run scripts/validate_company_sql.py --fix-headers to normalize local preview file titles before validation.
standard daily export writes adoption_status = 2. Non-daily candidates are not exported by default.
